title: castilla elastica subsp. gummifera
intro: Castilla elastica subsp. gummifera, also known as the Mexican rubber tree, is a plant species in the family Moraceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Castilla elastica subsp. gummifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are made from its bark, leaves, or latex.
